name: Change Kernel
summary: Owns the trusted Change state machine and produces content-addressed Accepted Change Packages.

invariants: Workers and submitted Evidence cannot ratify their own output.

Builtin, direct Gate, and authority-approved cross-Claim Evidence cover Claims only through their frozen compiler-owned Verification Obligation mapping; Gate Evidence issuance and consumption both require the exact eligible Gate and command route, and cross-Claim coverage additionally binds the exact target and an envelope source Claim.
Acceptance binds exact governance, repository content, Evidence, Closure, and authority.
Outcome alignment inputs and compiler-produced alignment are bound into the Verification Subject and Accepted Change Package.
Outcome exceptions require the frozen Development Plan authority and can never grant progress or transition proof.
Acceptance re-derives compiler-owned Change projections from the frozen Governance Baseline before sealing them.
Every Candidate freezes a content-addressed catalog of prior Accepted Packages; plan-amendment Transition proof is re-resolved before Gate execution and acceptance.
Within the local single-writer Change Store, a later historically Accepted Project Model amendment makes every earlier Candidate stale before compilation, Gate execution, or acceptance.
Declared Knowledge Gap Closure Contracts are rechecked before ordinary Change compilation, Gate execution, and acceptance; only the Outcome Transition compiler may authorize a Gap closure delta.
A Change id can produce at most one Accepted Package; invalidated historical acceptance requires a new follow-up Change.
Plan amendments cannot remove or reinterpret stable Outcome Criteria or add Criteria during an Outcome status transition.

Project Model owns strict planRefs value semantics; Change Kernel owns presence-based planRef alias conflicts and must adapt create and patch inputs before persistence so the Store contains only canonical planRefs.

Project and Change read queries fail closed unless at most three observations contain two consecutive identical source digests; a stabilized Change query observes Model, Git, and Store once per round, projects every record without writes, preserves historical state and acceptance, and returns bounded list summaries while full Evidence and package bodies remain detail-only.

An Architecture Profile query compiles one Project Model-owned Claim Gate route product covering every Contract Claim in the stabilized current Model, passes that exact process-local product into Profile compilation, and compiles at most one product per unique verified frozen Governance Baseline over the union of actually referenced non-builtin source Claims; one query-level ceiling charges each unique product's compiler-owned workUnits, routes, and totalRouteBytes plus bounded Kernel route projection work once, cache hits are not recharged, and operational observations never become assurance or Evidence.

One stabilized Workbench query reuses the Architecture Profile's current-Model route product to project the complete canonical owned and dependency Contract Claim choices; existing-Change compile, Gate, and accept actions project the complete canonical set allowed by the frozen Governance Baseline and Kernel lifecycle selection semantics, annotate exact Claim routes only from the compiler-owned Verification Plan, and do not prune an otherwise runnable Gate merely because it covers no Change Claim; every unavailable action emits the non-empty duplicate-free subset of change-kernel-interface workbenchDisabledReasonCodes in declared precedence order, and no adapter may reconstruct these decisions from raw Project Model or Change fields.
Integration requires current full assurance when policy demands it.
